visualizerParams.title: Large Non-Mersenne Prime
visualizerParams.description: Calculate the last ten digits of the massive prime number: 28433 * 2^7830457 + 1.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
def solution(n: int = 10) -> str:
    """
    Returns the last n digits of the prime number: 28433 * 2^7830457 + 1
    """
    # We only need the last 10 digits, so we work modulo 10^10
    modulus = 10**n
    
    # Use modular exponentiation: pow(base, exp, mod) is efficient
    power_part = pow(2, 7830457, modulus)
    
    # Complete the calculation under the modulus
    result = (28433 * power_part + 1) % modulus
    
    # Format with leading zeros if necessary
    return str(result).zfill(n)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"Last ten digits: {solution(10)}")
visualizerParams.finalCode:

    """

    # We only need the last 10 digits, so we work modulo 10^10

    power_part = pow(2, 7830457, modulus)
    
    # Complete the calculation under the modulus
    result = (28433 * power_part + 1) % modulus
    
    # Format with leading zeros if necessary
    return str(result).zfill(n)


if __name__ == "__main__":
